evidence: `decompress()` now returns `Result<Vec<u16>, String>` (decompress.rs line 12). `width.checked_mul(height)` overflow guard present (line 13–14); short-input guard returns `Err` instead of a zero-filled buffer (line 16–21). Call site in lib.rs line 232 uses `.map_err(|e| JsError::new(&e))?`. Failure mode eliminated.

`row_base` and `row2_base` are precomputed before the inner column loop (decompress.rs lines 29–30). All predictor index expressions in the north-west predictor block use these precomputed values (lines 71–77). Redundant per-iteration multiplications gone.
No property-based or round-trip tests were written. No ADR file exists for this task. Fix log for decompress-batch makes no mention of tests. Task remains unaddressed.

`demosaic_rggb` now returns `Result<Vec<u16>, String>` (demosaic.rs line 27). The `assert_eq!` that would have produced a WASM trap is gone; dimension mismatch returns `Err` (lines 34–41). Call site in lib.rs line 267 propagates via `map_err(|e| JsError::new(&e))?`.

`width.checked_mul(height)` guard added at demosaic.rs lines 28–31, returning `Err` on overflow before any multiplication result is used. The previously unchecked `width * height` multiply is gone from the assertion path.

Zero-dimension guard `if width == 0 || height == 0 { return Err(...) }` added at demosaic.rs lines 31–33, placed after the overflow check. Prevents `(width - 1)` and `(height - 1)` wrapping to `usize::MAX`.

`debug_assert!(r * stride + c < plane.len(), ...)` added immediately before the `get_unchecked` call (demosaic.rs line 13). Fires only in debug builds; zero overhead in release. The unsafe block remains but is now guarded in test/debug contexts.

Comments `// Transfers ownership; returns empty Vec<u8> on subsequent calls.` added to `take_rgb` (lib.rs line 106), `take_rgb16_lb` (line 117), and `take_rgb16_thumb` (line 122). The double-consume semantics are now documented; JS callers can read the contract from the generated bindings.
`strip_end` now computed with `.checked_add(...)` (lib.rs lines 223–225), returning `JsError` on overflow. The plain `+` that could silently wrap on wasm32 is gone.
Same fix as 002-logic-a1b2c3d4 — `checked_add` at lib.rs lines 223–225 covers both the logic overflow and the security overflow defeating the bounds guard. Failure mode eliminated.
`downscale_rgb` now has `if dst_w == 0 || dst_h == 0 { return Err(...) }` guard (lib.rs lines 381–383) placed before the f32 division. Division-by-zero panic eliminated.

In `downscale_rgb16_impl`, `y0`/`y1` are computed outside the `dx` loop (lib.rs lines 154–156) and `row_base = y * sw` is precomputed inside the `y` loop (line 164). The f32 row-bounds recomputation per output pixel is gone.

Comment `// 0xFFFF = absent/unknown (mirrors has_gps pattern using explicit bool for GPS)` added in the `ProcessResult` struct definition (lib.rs line 84) adjacent to the `wb_mode` field. The undocumented sentinel is now documented in source.

Both the `dst_w == 0 || dst_h == 0` guard (lib.rs lines 381–383) and the `checked_mul` length check (lines 376–378) prevent division-by-zero in `downscale_rgb`. No `f32` Inf/NaN can be produced from a zero dimension.
`sw * sh * 3` replaced with `sw.checked_mul(sh).and_then(|n| n.checked_mul(3)).ok_or_else(...)` (lib.rs lines 376–378). Overflow-safe; returns `JsError` instead of a wrapped comparison.
Same fix as 002-errors-y7z8a9 — `checked_mul` chain at lib.rs lines 376–378 removes the overflow that would let the length guard pass incorrectly for attacker-controlled large dimensions.

Comment `// Falls back to built-in CAM_TO_SRGB if caller passes wrong-length slice.` added above the `if color_matrix_flat.len() == 9` branch (lib.rs line 463). Silent fallback is now documented at the call site.

`apply_look` now computes `expected_pixels = (width as usize).checked_mul(height as usize)` and checks `n != expected_pixels * 3`, returning `JsError` on mismatch (lib.rs lines 446–453). Buffer-length against declared dimensions is validated.

`apply_look` unpacks bytes via `rgb16_bytes.chunks_exact(2).map(|b| u16::from_le_bytes([b[0], b[1]])).collect()` (lib.rs lines 454–457), replacing the previous index-based per-element map. The iterator is allocation-equivalent but avoids the per-element `get`.
Covered by same `checked_mul` + length mismatch guard added to `apply_look` (lib.rs lines 446–453) as 002-security-p1q2r3s4. Width/height from JS are now validated against `rgb16_bytes.len()`.

Comment `// Rational fields use 0/0 as absent-sentinel (JS checks den==0 before dividing).` added above the exposure/fnumber/focal_length field initialisation (lib.rs line 347). The cross-language convention is now documented in source.
Comment `// Input must be a multiple of 3 bytes; trailing bytes are ignored.` added above `rgb_to_rgba` (lib.rs line 498). Truncation of trailing partial pixels is now documented.

`rgb_to_rgba` now uses `rgb.chunks_exact(3)` + `out.extend_from_slice(&[chunk[0], chunk[1], chunk[2], 255])` (lib.rs lines 503–505), replacing per-byte pushes. `Vec::with_capacity` pre-allocates correctly.
No machine-readable WASM API schema or ADR was written. The fix logs contain only code changes; no ADR file exists in the review directory. Task remains unaddressed.
No structured logging channel was added and no ADR was written. Fix logs do not mention this task. Task remains unaddressed.
No SAST tooling (cargo-audit, cargo-geiger, Clippy deny) was configured in CI and no ADR was written. Task remains unaddressed.
No unit tests for the double-consume edge case of `take_rgb`/`take_rgb16_lb`/`take_rgb16_thumb` were added and no ADR was written. Task remains unaddressed.

Timing instrumentation was not extended to cover `downscale_rgb16_impl` calls or `apply_unsharp_masks`, and no ADR was written. The existing `decompress_ms`/`demosaic_ms`/`tonemap_ms`/`orient_ms` fields are unchanged.
No centralized input validation schema for ORF structural constraints was created and no ADR was written. Task remains unaddressed.
No typed error enum or error taxonomy was introduced; all errors remain unstructured strings, and no ADR was written. Task remains unaddressed.

Inline-vs-pointer discrimination is still duplicated across `as_u32`, `as_ascii`, `parse_olympus_makernote`, and `parse_image_processing_subifd`. No shared utility helper was extracted and no ADR was written.
No LUT caching mechanism was implemented; the comment added to `linear_to_srgb` documents the opportunity but no ADR was written. LUTs are still rebuilt on every `process()` call.
No WASM SIMD implementation via `std::arch::wasm32` was attempted and no ADR was written. Task remains unaddressed.

Inline SHORT[2] WB level extraction now uses bit-shift on the already-decoded `val` u32: LE uses `(val & 0xFFFF, val >> 16)`, BE uses `(val >> 16, val & 0xFFFF)` (tiff.rs lines 517–521). The unconditional `to_le_bytes()` that broke BE files is gone.

`IfdEntry::as_u32` dtype==3 branch changed from `self.count == 1` to `self.count <= 2` (tiff.rs line 238), with a comment explaining the inline layout. count==2 inline SHORTs are now read from the value field instead of treated as a file pointer.

`as_ascii` now uses `start.checked_add(self.count as usize)` with a bounds check against `r.data.len()` (tiff.rs lines 260–263), returning `String::new()` on overflow or OOB. The plain `(value_off + count) as usize` that could overflow is gone.
Same fix as 002-errors-p7q8r9 — `checked_add` at tiff.rs lines 260–263 covers both the logic and security overflow in `as_ascii`.
`read_ifd` caps `count` at 512 with `.min(512)` before `Vec::with_capacity` (tiff.rs line 297). Attacker-controlled IFD entry counts can no longer trigger unbounded ~1.3 MB allocations per call.

All three sub-IFD dispatch calls in `parse_olympus_makernote` (tags 0x2010, 0x2020, 0x2040) replaced `base_off as u32 + val` with `(base_off as u32).checked_add(val).unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` (tiff.rs lines 362–363, 367–368, 419–420). Overflow-wrapping sub-IFD offset eliminated.

Fixed as part of 002-logic-c9d0e1f2 — same bug site. The `to_le_bytes()` that produced wrong WB values on BE ORF files is replaced by the endianness-aware bit-shift extraction (tiff.rs lines 517–521).

The fixer assessed the silent `if let Ok(exif) = read_ifd(...)` pattern as intentional (partial EXIF is acceptable per design) and made no change. No ADR was written. The finding remains unaddressed by design; this is a deliberate non-fix, not an oversight.
Covered by the `.min(512)` cap in `read_ifd` (tiff.rs line 297) — same fix as 002-errors-m4n5o6. The DoS via attacker-controlled Vec allocation is bounded.

Sub-IFD offset arithmetic for tags 0x2010, 0x2020, 0x2040 now uses `checked_add(...).unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` (tiff.rs lines 362–363, 367–368, 419–420). The unchecked u32 addition that could overflow is gone.

`separable_blur_into` helper extracted with `[0f32; 3]` accumulator pattern (pipeline.rs lines 180–200); `separable_blur` uses it for the horizontal pass (line 208); vertical pass uses same channel-array pattern (lines 213–228). Single `scratch` Vec allocated once in `apply_unsharp_masks` and reused across both passes (lines 253–261). Clamp bug fixed from `0..4095` to `0..65535` (line 245).

The fixer applied `#[inline(always)]` to `linear_to_srgb` and added a comment about JS-side caching, but the LUTs are still rebuilt on every `process()` call (pipeline.rs lines 275–286). The finding was about rebuilding three 65536-entry pre-LUTs and one post-LUT at interactive slider rates — that remains unchanged. `#[inline(always)]` reduces call overhead within the LUT build loop but does not eliminate the rebuild cost. Partial improvement only.
No LUT caching across `apply_look` calls was implemented. A comment documents the opportunity (pipeline.rs lines 83–86) but no ADR was written for this adr_draft task.

The fixer correctly assessed that powf is LLVM-intrinsified on wasm32 and the `#[inline(always)]` on `linear_to_srgb` is the correct fix (pipeline.rs line 76). The finding's own conclusion was 'keep powf'; failure mode was excessive call overhead, which inlining addresses.

Fixed as part of 002-performance-a1b2c3d4. Both horizontal (`separable_blur_into`) and vertical passes of `separable_blur` now use `[0f32; 3]` accumulators with explicit per-channel indexing inside the kernel loop (pipeline.rs lines 185–196, 214–226), enabling LLVM vectorization across channels.

`apply_unsharp_mask_with_scratch` shares a single `scratch` Vec across both texture and clarity passes (pipeline.rs lines 253–261). However, inside each call the function still invokes `separable_blur(rgb16, ...)` which allocates its own `temp + out` buffers internally (lines 239, 211–229) — the `scratch` written by `separable_blur_into` is unused for the actual blur result. Peak simultaneous allocation is still 3 full-image buffers per call (scratch + temp + out inside separable_blur), not the 2 claimed. This is a partial improvement (scratch allocation is amortized across calls) but the core allocation pattern is not resolved.

`vib_zero = vib.abs() < 1e-6` hoisted before the pixel loop (pipeline.rs line 291). Inside the loop, `pixel_sat` and `vib_w` computations are skipped when `vib_zero` is true (lines 311–321). The four max/min/div/clamp operations are avoided for every pixel when vibrance is zero.
No WASM SIMD implementation was attempted and no ADR was written for this adr_draft task.

Doc comment added to `apply_orientation` (pipeline.rs lines 376–378) enumerating handled orientations (3, 6, 8) and documenting that 1, 2, 4, 5, 7 pass through as-is with the Olympus ORF rationale. Silent pass-through is now documented.

`rotate_180` inner loop now uses `dst_row[c*3..c*3+3].copy_from_slice(&s_row[sc*3..sc*3+3])` (pipeline.rs line 454). The three individual byte assignments are replaced by a 3-byte slice copy, allowing the compiler to emit a word-load/store or memcpy intrinsic.
